Title: v0.2.2 — Shader tabs, dirty guards, two-way save toasts
Summary: Shader edit page split into Code + Live preview tabs (bigger canvas, aspect ratio chips, pause/scrub time). Comprehensive unsaved-form guard with confirm modal across every Edit page. Bidirectional save toasts with a cyan/emerald color split. Linux desktop integration (`pnpm install:desktop`). New app icon. Five new body fonts. Stable-state version marker.
Body:
## Headlines ### Shader edit page split into two tabs The shader editor was previously a `lg:grid-cols-[1fr_22rem]` split: CodeMirror on the left, a 22rem sidebar on the right cramming the WebGL preview canvas + uniforms + card style + usages all together. Live preview canvas ended up locked at 1:1 in a ~256px sidebar — too small to inspect a real shader. v0.2.2 splits the page into **Code** and **Live preview** tabs. Both stay mounted across switches (the `hidden` attribute, not unmounting) so: - CodeMirror scroll + history survives flipping back and forth. - WebGL2 context stays alive — no recompile lag or texture re-upload on every flip. - Uniform values + time-control state persist. Tab badges: the Code tab shows an amber dot when the buffer is dirty; the Live preview tab shows a red dot when there are parse or compile errors. The active tab is persisted to `localStorage` so you land on the same tab on next visit. The **Live preview tab toolbar** adds two control clusters: - **Aspect ratio chips** — `1:1` / `16:9` / `4:3` / `21:9`. Default 16:9 (most game viewports), persisted globally (per-app, not per-shader — your monitor and game viewport ratio stay constant regardless of which shader you're editing). - **Pause / Play / Scrub time** — every shader is fundamentally time-based (`u_time` is one of the injected built-ins), but at 60Hz it's hard to inspect a *specific moment* of a time-driven effect (decay, trails, moving patterns). Pause freezes `u_time` at its current value; the 0..60s scrub slider lets you drag to any specific time; ↺ resets to 0. Scrubbing while playing implicitly pauses. Resume re-anchors the runtime's startTime so playback continues at the scrubbed value with no visual jump. A live `u_time` readout sits between the buttons (polls `runtime.getCurrentTime()` once per animation frame, formatted to 1 decimal so it doesn't strobe). ### Unsaved-form guard everywhere with a confirm modal Every Edit page that has a Save button is now guarded against losing unsaved work: - **DirtyDot** — a tiny shared amber-`●` component renders next to the page title whenever the buffer diverges from the last-saved baseline. Standardized across every Edit page (8 game-domain + concept + codex entry + codex category + Preferences/Godot project). - **`useUnsavedWarning`** — now covers two surfaces. (1) **Browser events**: window close / refresh / AppImage quit via `beforeunload`. (2) **In-app navigation**: clicking a sidebar link, hitting Back, any `` that changes pathname. When blocked, a pixel-themed `showConfirm` modal asks *"Discard unsaved changes?"* with **Discard and leave** or **Keep editing**. Same-path navigation (e.g. tweaking a search param on the same page) is not blocked. - **ImageEditor modal-close guard** — the image editor isn't a route, so `useBlocker` doesn't apply. It got its own `attemptClose()` wrapper intercepting all four close paths (Escape, backdrop click, header ✕, Cancel button) with a derived `isDirty` signal (undo stack length + crop rect + tint dial values) and the same confirm modal. > note: this required migrating the app shell from declarative `` to a data router (`createBrowserRouter` + `RouterProvider`) — `useBlocker` requires a data-router context. The migration is mechanical; every existing URL still resolves to the same component. ### Bidirectional save toasts with color-coded direction The **outgoing-save toast bridge** rounds out toast feedback to both directions: - **Incoming** (Godot → Bleepforge): emerald `success` toast, title *"<Domain> updated externally"* - **Outgoing** (Bleepforge → disk): cyan `saved` toast, title *"Saved <domain>"* - **Failed outgoing**: red `error` toast with the server's message - **Warning outcomes** (writer flagged something — e.g. orphan ext_resource cleanup): amber `warn` toast Color *and* title both encode direction so the distinction works for color-blind users too. **SaveDomain extended**: `concept`, `codex-entry`, `codex-category` join the eight game-domain entries. These are Bleepforge-only manual Save buttons that now show up in the Saves audit feed AND fire the new outgoing-save toast. **Five discrete theme actions** push toasts inline from `GlobalTheme.tsx`: switch / create / delete global themes, and create / delete custom color themes. Continuous direct manipulation (color override picks, font/size/spacing slider drags) deliberately stays silent — would flood the toast surface if added. ### Linux desktop integration: `pnpm install:desktop` Fedora's `kio-extras` is built without `libappimage`, so Dolphin couldn't render AppImage file thumbnails on its own. **`pnpm install:desktop`** is a one-shot script that does four user-local integrations — no system packages, no sudo: 1. Copies hicolor icon sizes (16 → 1024) to `~/.local/share/icons/hicolor//apps/bleepforge.png`. 2. Writes `~/.local/share/applications/bleepforge.desktop` pointing at the newest AppImage in `release/`. 3. Installs a generic AppImage XDG `.thumbnailer` (works for any AppImage on the system, not just Bleepforge). 4. **Pre-caches thumbnails per the freedesktop Thumbnail Managing Standard** — 8-bit PNG with proper `tEXt` chunks at the URI-keyed location. This is the only mechanism that actually works for Dolphin without libappimage. After the script runs, Bleepforge shows in the KDE menu / KRunner / GNOME activities with the proper icon, and AppImage files in your file manager render their embedded icon. Fully close + reopen Dolphin after first install — F5 isn't enough, Dolphin caches thumbnail-lookup decisions in-process. ### New app icon Replaced the previous anvil + speech-balloon + sparks mark with a **single emerald speech bubble + center blip**. Same forge concept (the wordmark BLEEPFORGE carries the verb; the icon carries the noun), but one element instead of three. Reads cleanly at every size from 16px up to 1024px because pixel-grid alignment was the load-bearing constraint from the start: the 4px outline survives nearest-neighbor downscale to 16px as a clean 1-pixel line, and the 4×4 blip becomes exactly one pixel at 16px (1:64 source ratio). Splash + window + AppImage thumbnail all updated. ## Polish + micro-interactions - **Five new body fonts** — Major Mono Display, Iceland, Monoton, Bungee, Faster One. Brings the total to 13 picked to widen the aesthetic palette beyond pixel-only. - **State-aware restart icon** — the sidebar restart shifts to emerald with a slow opacity-pulse glow whenever the running server's boot-captured config differs from saved preferences. Today the only trigger is the Godot project root delta; future per-domain folder overrides will OR into the same hook. - **Toast bounce-in entry** — every newly-pushed toast animates in via a `cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)` keyframe that briefly overshoots past resting position before settling. 220ms, transform + opacity only (compositor-only, never repaints). - **Theme swap cross-fade** — flipping the active theme adds a `theme-transitioning` class to `` for 220ms; while present, color-bearing properties carry a 200ms transition so the swap fades instead of snaps. Scoped to the transition window so normal hovers stay snappy. - **Universal card hover lift** — every list-page card (Npc / Item / Quest / Karma / Faction / Balloon / Shader / Asset) translates up by 2px on hover via the shared `.card-lift` utility class. Compositor-only transform. - **Pixel-art loading skeletons** — replaced ~30 inline `
Loading…
` placeholders across every list / Edit page with the new `PixelSkeleton` component (3 stacked rectangle bars with a staggered pulse-opacity animation that waves top to bottom). - **Help popout enlarged** — bumped from 1100×800 to **1400×950** after first daily use felt cramped. Help ships the persistent 2-column layout + prose bodies that benefit from a wider reading column. - **Plus-version cleanup on Edit page navigation** — after saving and navigating away, the dirty state flips clean *before* the navigate call so the guard's blocker sees clean state and doesn't prompt. ## Under the hood - **Data router migration** — App.tsx is now the layout element of a `createBrowserRouter` config (route table lives in main.tsx); App renders an `` where the active route mounts. Required because `useBlocker` (backing the in-app navigation guard) only works in a data-router context. Every URL still resolves to the same component; only the wiring changed. - **Stable-state version marker** — Sidebar version chip + Splash subtitle now detect any semver pre-release suffix on `__APP_VERSION__` (anything containing a `-`, e.g. `0.2.3-dev`) and switch color from neutral to amber with a tooltip *"Development build — last stable: vX"*. The "last stable" string is sourced from a custom `lastStable` field on `electron/package.json`, wired through Vite's `define` as `__LAST_STABLE_VERSION__`. - **Generic AppImage thumbnailer** — the bash script installed by `pnpm install:desktop` uses the AppImage's own `--appimage-extract '.DirIcon'` command (no `libappimage` required) to pull the embedded icon. Crucial bash detail: `.DirIcon` is extracted as a *broken* symlink (its target wasn't extracted in the same pass), so `[ -e ]` returns false. Use `[ -L ]` first, readlink the target, re-extract that specific path. - **Shared domain → label + route map** — extracted from `syncToasts.ts` into `lib/saves/domainLabels.ts` so both toast bridges (incoming + outgoing) use identical metadata. - **Renderer teardown** — same `pagehide` cleanup pattern (closes EventSources + BroadcastChannels) that prevents the Chromium SIGTRAP on window close. Already shipped pre-v0.2.2; mentioned here because two new channels (saves + custom-theme broadcast) ride on the same teardown. ## Known issues / deferred - **Windows + macOS packaging** still not configured in electron-builder.json. v0.2.3's primary scope is the Windows build for an external tester. - **Auto-update + code signing** still deferred until distribution is something other than "the user runs the AppImage from disk." - **Genericize for any Godot project** — long-term anchor (v0.2.5+). The seven game-domain schemas + per-domain edit forms + per-domain `.tres` mappers are still hardcoded to Flock of Bleeps. > tip: see [v0.2.1](/help/release-notes/v0-2-1) for the custom themes + sidebar shell + splash work this release builds on.
